text.title: Agent Integration for Generative UI Design
text.summary: Agent-native generation path, iterative refinement pattern, service catalog, and gotchas.

rule.statement: Use Claude Artifacts for agent-loop iteration — it is the only tool where the agent can see and modify output without a human browser session.
rule.rationale: Galileo has no public API and exports require a human in a Figma plugin. v0 API is not GA as of 2026. Claude Artifacts is generated inline in the agent response and immediately visible to both agent and human.

rule.statement: Document why a variant was rejected — this becomes the signal for better next prompts.
rule.rationale: Non-deterministic output means the same prompt may yield structurally different results across runs. Rejection reasons make prompt improvements systematic rather than trial-and-error.

list.item: Claude Artifacts (SaaS) — most agent-native; agent generates artifact inline, no separate API call
v0 by Vercel (SaaS) — CLI available; API in beta as of 2026; Tailwind + shadcn/ui only
Galileo AI (SaaS) — UI-only, no public API, Figma plugin for export
Uizard (SaaS) — project API in beta; browser-based primary UX
Builder.io (SaaS) — REST + SDK; can receive AI-generated specs and render visually
axe-cli — accessibility audit on rendered generated UI
shadcn/ui CLI — scaffold component library for testing generated code

antipattern.description: Using fetch(), localStorage, or external assets in Claude Artifacts previews.
antipattern.reason: Claude Artifacts previews are sandboxed — no fetch(), no localStorage, no external assets. Generated code using these will appear broken in preview. Use static data for prototypes.

antipattern.description: Relying on reproducibility of generative tools.
antipattern.reason: The same prompt across two runs may yield structurally different components. Do not treat any single generative output as a canonical reference.

text.reference: Structured prompt for React + Tailwind UI generation
Batch v0 CLI generation from a prompts file
